pull.title: musig: clear `pubnonce` output for invalid `seckey`
pull.body:
This is a follow-up to PR #1849.

### Problem
`secp256k1_musig_nonce_gen` returns failure if its [arguments are invalid](https://github.com/bitcoin-core/secp256k1/blob/b11340b3ce2afac1b6ffda4ce5828c30621d2917/include/secp256k1_musig.h#L337), for example when a non-NULL [`seckey`](https://github.com/bitcoin-core/secp256k1/blob/b11340b3ce2afac1b6ffda4ce5828c30621d2917/include/secp256k1_musig.h#L347-L348) fails [secret-key validation](https://github.com/bitcoin-core/secp256k1/blob/b11340b3ce2afac1b6ffda4ce5828c30621d2917/src/modules/musig/session_impl.h#L391).

On that path, `secp256k1_musig_nonce_gen_internal` invalidates the [secret nonce](https://github.com/bitcoin-core/secp256k1/blob/b11340b3ce2afac1b6ffda4ce5828c30621d2917/src/modules/musig/session_impl.h#L414) on failure, but still unconditionally saves the derived [public nonce output](https://github.com/bitcoin-core/secp256k1/blob/b11340b3ce2afac1b6ffda4ce5828c30621d2917/src/modules/musig/session_impl.h#L433) before returning failure.

### Fix
Clear `pubnonce` before returning the invalid-`seckey` failure, so a failed nonce-generation call does not leave a newly written `pubnonce` behind.

The implementation still saves the public nonce before clearing it on failure (instead of branching around the save), to preserve constant-time control flow with respect to the secret-key validation result.

The second commit only extends existing MuSig API failure-path coverage for paths where `secp256k1_musig_nonce_gen_internal` already clears `pubnonce` at function entry before any of its own validation steps.

repo.name: secp256k1
repo.description: Optimized C library for EC operations on curve secp256k1

events.body:
Concept ACK

AFAICT this pattern matches what we do in similar API functions (e.g. [`_schnorrsig_sign32`](https://github.com/bitcoin-core/secp256k1/blob/b11340b3ce2afac1b6ffda4ce5828c30621d2917/src/modules/schnorrsig/main_impl.h#L181), [`_keypair_create`](https://github.com/bitcoin-core/secp256k1/blob/b11340b3ce2afac1b6ffda4ce5828c30621d2917/src/modules/extrakeys/main_impl.h#L208), [`_ellswift_create`](https://github.com/bitcoin-core/secp256k1/blob/b11340b3ce2afac1b6ffda4ce5828c30621d2917/src/modules/ellswift/main_impl.h#L464)).

verification.payload:
tree 45b026d03c437f6521af7db4dd95da5ed58201ca
parent b11340b3ce2afac1b6ffda4ce5828c30621d2917
author Lőrinc <pap.lorinc@gmail.com> 1777451254 +0200
committer Lőrinc <pap.lorinc@gmail.com> 1777484351 +0200

musig: test cleared nonce outputs on failures

`secp256k1_musig_nonce_gen_internal` clears `pubnonce` at the top of the function, before the later validation steps covered here.

Extend the existing MuSig API failure-path checks for `nonce_gen` and `nonce_gen_counter` to assert that these paths leave the public nonce output cleared.

musig: test cleared nonce outputs on failures

`secp256k1_musig_nonce_gen_internal` clears `pubnonce` at the top of the function, before the later validation steps covered here.

Extend the existing MuSig API failure-path checks for `nonce_gen` and `nonce_gen_counter` to assert that these paths leave the public nonce output cleared.

verification.payload:
tree ec5da18f2b6c83f2b1ecc0f26d18242294cb368c
parent bd3859827ef3f7c862f211702e480b718ec0a44d
author Lőrinc <pap.lorinc@gmail.com> 1777451227 +0200
committer Lőrinc <pap.lorinc@gmail.com> 1777484351 +0200

musig: clear pubnonce output for invalid seckey

`secp256k1_musig_nonce_gen` returns failure for invalid arguments, including a non-NULL `seckey` that fails validation.

`secp256k1_musig_nonce_gen_internal` already invalidates `secnonce` on that failure, but it still saves derived nonce points into `pubnonce` before returning.

Clear `pubnonce` with a conditional clear after the save, so the cleanup stays branchless with respect to the secret-key validation result and reverting this cleanup leaves stale derived nonce data in the MuSig API test.

musig: clear pubnonce output for invalid seckey

`secp256k1_musig_nonce_gen` returns failure for invalid arguments, including a non-NULL `seckey` that fails validation.

`secp256k1_musig_nonce_gen_internal` already invalidates `secnonce` on that failure, but it still saves derived nonce points into `pubnonce` before returning.

Clear `pubnonce` with a conditional clear after the save, so the cleanup stays branchless with respect to the secret-key validation result and reverting this cleanup leaves stale derived nonce data in the MuSig API test.

events.body:
> On that path, `secp256k1_musig_nonce_gen_internal` invalidates the [secret nonce](https://github.com/bitcoin-core/secp256k1/blob/b11340b3ce2afac1b6ffda4ce5828c30621d2917/src/modules/musig/session_impl.h#L414) on failure, but still unconditionally saves the derived [public nonce output](https://github.com/bitcoin-core/secp256k1/blob/b11340b3ce2afac1b6ffda4ce5828c30621d2917/src/modules/musig/session_impl.h#L433) before returning failure.

Why is that a problem?

> > On that path, `secp256k1_musig_nonce_gen_internal` invalidates the [secret nonce](https://github.com/bitcoin-core/secp256k1/blob/b11340b3ce2afac1b6ffda4ce5828c30621d2917/src/modules/musig/session_impl.h#L414) on failure, but still unconditionally saves the derived [public nonce output](https://github.com/bitcoin-core/secp256k1/blob/b11340b3ce2afac1b6ffda4ce5828c30621d2917/src/modules/musig/session_impl.h#L433) before returning failure.
> 
> Why is that a problem?

From a reviewer's perspective, I see this PR as a small consistency improvement, fully enforcing the (unwritten?) rule "if any of the input parameters are invalid or the function fails for any other reason, all of the out parameters are zeroed/invalidated". Probably not an actual problem in practice though, considering that users should always check for return values before evaluating out-parameters in the first place.

comments.body: in fde940f38a0ddaa3c4b556eeff42bcd3ed89c319: nit: I suspect this change was meant to be part of the next commit?
No, this is the one that reproduces the fix (it fails without the fix), the next commit adds these checks that were already correct. Would it help if I moved that commit first?
Ah, I see now. Swapping commits would indeed help to clarify that I think, but happy to (re-)ACK in either order.
